**Properties (LIVE SCHEMA):**

- Speaker (rich_text): Guest/creator name
- Channel (rich_text): Podcast/channel name

- Date Watched (date): When processed/extracted
- Status (select): Queued, Watching, Extracted, Archived

- Notes (rich_text): Freeform notes
- Change Log (rich_text): All changes tracked

---


### Step 2: Get Transcript

**Priority order:**
1. Manual subtitles (most accurate)
2. Auto-generated captions
3. Whisper fallback (ask user first - requires downloading audio)

```bash
# List available subs
yt-dlp --list-subs "VIDEO_URL"

# Try manual subs first
yt-dlp --write-sub --sub-lang en --skip-download -o "transcript" "VIDEO_URL"

# Fallback to auto subs
yt-dlp --write-auto-sub --sub-lang en --skip-download -o "transcript" "VIDEO_URL"
```

**If no captions available:** Use the video description (often contains detailed takeaways, chapters, and key points). Also fetch the episode page if linked in description using WebFetch.


### Step 3: Application Analysis

For EACH of these three, write specific, actionable applications:

**Ryan Magin:**
- Which frameworks apply to his personal brand, real estate content, authority positioning
- Specific use cases for his filming, speaking, offer creation

**Drey:**
- Which frameworks validate or sharpen Drey's existing methodology
- Personal brand applications
- How this connects to Drey's content strategy

**Viral Editz:**
- Client positioning applications
- Team training applications
- Service delivery / VES editing directives
- Content strategy implications


### Step 5: Temporal Analysis & Automation Filter

**This step is MANDATORY.** Check the video's upload_date and assess every tactic mentioned.

**5a. Date Context**
Note the upload date prominently. Flag the age:
- < 1 year old: Current. Tactics likely still valid.
- 1-3 years old: Check tactics. Principles valid, tools/platforms may have changed.
- 3+ years old: Principles gold. Tactics need full audit. Tools likely outdated.

**5b. Principle vs Tactic Split**
For EVERY actionable recommendation in the video, classify:
- **PRINCIPLE (Timeless):** Human psychology, persuasion, storytelling, communication patterns, decision-making frameworks. These don't expire. Mark as "Timeless."
- **TACTIC (Time-Bound):** Specific platform features, software tools, growth hacks, algorithm strategies, pricing models, website references. These expire. Mark as "Check Current."

**5c. Automation Filter (Drey's Actual Stack)**
For every MANUAL tactic mentioned, ask: "Is there an AI/automation tool that does this now?" Then give the EXACT tool, EXACT setup, and how the clone executes it. No vague suggestions -- specific tools Drey owns or can access.

**Drey's Live Stack (clone can use all of these):**
- Claude Code + MCP (Notion API, Google Drive API) -- agentic execution
- Notion (full second brain, all databases, API access)
- Google Drive (file storage, content delivery)
- ElevenLabs (voice cloning, TTS API)
- HeyGen (avatar video API)
- Publer (auto-posting API, 13 platforms)
- Make.com (workflow automation, connects everything)
- yt-dlp (video/audio download + transcript extraction)
- Plaud/Limitless (audio capture + transcription)
- ManyChat (Instagram DM automation)
- Midjourney / DALL-E / Higgsfield (image generation)
- Perplexity / Claude / ChatGPT (research + content generation)
- Framer AI / v0.dev (landing pages)
- Opus Clip (auto-clipping long videos into shorts)

**Automation Upgrade Map (use YOUR tools, not generic suggestions):**

| Old Manual Tactic | Modern Version (Drey's Stack) | Clone Executes How |
|-------------------|------------------------------|-------------------|
| "DM people individually" | ManyChat flows on Instagram | Set up keyword triggers + auto-response sequences |
| "Write email sequences" | Claude Code generates copy > Make.com sends via ConvertKit | Notion content idea > Claude writes > auto-sends |
| "Edit videos manually" | Viral Editz NRVES + Opus Clip for auto-clips | Feed long video > Opus clips shorts > NRVES polishes |
| "Research competitors/topics" | Claude Code + Perplexity agents | Clone runs research agent, routes findings to Notion |
| "Schedule posts manually" | Publer API via Make.com | Video in Google Drive > Make triggers > Publer posts |
| "Transcribe interviews" | yt-dlp auto-subs or Plaud recordings | Automatic -- already in pipeline |
| "Create thumbnails" | Midjourney / DALL-E via API | Clone generates 3 options, picks highest-clarity one |
| "Build landing pages" | Framer AI or v0.dev | Clone builds from Notion content, deploys in minutes |
| "Cold outreach one by one" | Instantly.ai or Smartlead sequences | Upload list > AI writes personalized sequences > auto-sends |
| "Track analytics in spreadsheets" | Notion databases + Publer analytics API | Auto-populates via Make.com webhooks |
| "Film content yourself" | ElevenLabs voice + HeyGen avatar | Content idea > script > voice clone > avatar video > Drive |
| "Write scripts from scratch" | Claude Code reads Notion frameworks > generates script | Clone pulls relevant frameworks + Council data > writes |
| "Post content daily" | Full clone pipeline (Notion > ElevenLabs > HeyGen > Publer) | 100% hands-free. Approve in morning, pipeline runs |
| "Create system prompts" | YouTube Pipeline Route 6 auto-generates "[Speaker] Asks YOU" bots | Every video extraction = new shareable prompt |
| "Manually extract book insights" | Book Ingestion skill | Drop book > Claude extracts > routes to all databases |
| "Search your notes for ideas" | Notion API queries via Claude Code | Clone searches Council + Frameworks + Quotes for patterns |
| "Create offers manually" | Maria Wendt Coach skill + Notion content library | Clone builds offer from existing content + frameworks |

**IMPORTANT: Always give the specific tool + specific action. Never say "use AI" generically.**
Wrong: "You could use AI to automate this."
Right: "Clone runs Claude Code > reads Content & Ideas DB > generates script via ElevenLabs API > creates avatar video via HeyGen API > uploads to Google Drive 'Ready to Post' folder > Make.com triggers Publer auto-post to Instagram, TikTok, YouTube."

**5d. Output Format**
Include in the YouTube Library page content AND Route 1 entry:

```
TEMPORAL ANALYSIS
- Video Published: [DATE]
- Content Age: [X years/months]
- Overall Assessment: [Principles timeless / Tactics current / Tactics need update]

TIMELESS PRINCIPLES (still apply):
- [Principle 1]: [Why it's timeless]
- [Principle 2]: [Why it's timeless]

TACTICS TO VERIFY (may be outdated):
- [Tactic]: [What speaker said] > [Current status] > [Modern alternative if outdated]

AUTOMATION UPGRADES (with exact execution):
- [Manual tactic] > NOW USE: [Exact tool from Drey's stack] > HOW: [Specific steps the clone takes]
```

**Why this matters for the clone:**
- The clone needs to know WHEN advice was given to weight it correctly
- Principles from 2018 are as valid as 2026. Tactics from 2018 may be harmful
- The automation filter ensures the clone always executes the MODERN way using Drey's actual tools
- Every extraction makes the clone smarter about what's current vs what's legacy
- The clone never recommends manual work when automation exists in the stack
- This makes the entire knowledge base self-correcting over time

### Route 3: Master Frameworks + Council Framework Library

**Master Frameworks Database ID:** `5553912e-1355-4033-a743-90ff7d133e72`

Every video extraction identifies ALL frameworks mentioned. They get tiered:

**Tier 1 → Master Frameworks DB (Novel, Actionable)**
The framework is NOVEL if you can answer YES to: "If I removed the speaker's name, would this framework still be distinct from everything else in the database?"

Tier 1 criteria (must meet ALL):
- Speaker coined it, named it, or assembled something genuinely new
- Has specific steps, components, or a unique structure
- Actionable: someone can APPLY it, not just understand it
- Not a well-known principle repackaged (Halo Effect, loss aversion, first mover advantage, etc.)

For each Tier 1 framework, create an entry in Master Frameworks:
- Entity Name (title): Framework name (attributed to speaker)
- Entity Type (select): "Framework"
- Layer (select): Underground/Machinery/Surface
- Domains (multi_select): Relevant domains
- Description (rich_text): Full description with application context
- Source URL (url): YouTube link
- Change Log (rich_text): "[DATE]: Extracted from [Video Title] via YouTube Pipeline."

**Freshness Protocol (MANDATORY on every framework entry):**
Append this block to the page content of every framework created:
```
FRESHNESS PROTOCOL
- Source Release Date: [Video upload date]
- Classification: [Timeless Principle / Time-Bound Tactic / Hybrid]
- Automation Check: [Can this be automated with Drey's stack? If yes, how]
- Platform Dependency: [None / Platform-specific — name the platform]
```

**Tier 2 → Council Page Content (Known Principles Applied)**
Everything else — well-known principles the speaker cited, reframed, or applied. These are valuable as CLONE TRAINING DATA for the speaker's expert profile but don't need standalone database entries.

For Tier 2, append a section to the speaker's Council page:
- Heading: "How [Speaker] Thinks — Full Framework Library"
- Each framework gets a heading_3 + paragraph description
- Include how the speaker specifically applies/reframes the principle
- Tag with Change Log noting source video

**Why this matters for the clone vision:**
- Master Frameworks = YOUR clone's decision-making toolkit (high signal, no noise)
- Council Framework Libraries = EXPERT clone training data (comprehensive profiles)
- Your clone consults expert clones. Expert clones need maximum data to think like the original.
- Zero data loss. Maximum signal in both places.

**Expected output per video:**
- 2-4 Tier 1 entries in Master Frameworks (truly novel)
- 3-8 Tier 2 entries appended to Council page (known principles applied)
- Every framework mentioned is captured SOMEWHERE


### Route 5: Content & Ideas (MANDATORY - Content Fuel for Ryan + Drey)

**Database ID:** `6743487a-7863-473f-878c-fbad86ae18ef`
**Data Source ID:** `f0dbb615-5dd0-402a-8ecb-fb5240828c17`

Every YouTube extraction MUST generate content ideas for Ryan Magin and Drey. This is what makes the pipeline compound - every video watched becomes content to film.

**Generate 3-5 content ideas per person (Ryan + Drey separately).**

For each idea, create an entry with:
- Content Title (title): The video topic/hook
- Creator (multi_select): ["Ryan"] or ["Drey"] depending on who it's for
- Type (select): "Video Idea"
- Hook/Angle (rich_text): The opening hook for the video
- Hook (rich_text): Exact verbatim hook to open with
- Hook Options (rich_text): 3 hook variations. Format: Hook 1: [text] | Hook 2: [text] | Hook 3: [text]
- Big Idea (rich_text): One sentence - what's the point of this video
- Core Message (rich_text): The key insight from the source video being applied
- Script/Content (rich_text): Brief outline or talking points
- Source URL (url): YouTube link of the source video
- Source Type (select): "Video Idea"
- Bucket (select): Best matching YAP bucket (Life Advice, Controversial Take, Self-Improvement, Money/Wealth, etc.)
- Domains (multi_select): Relevant domains
- Platform (multi_select): Where to post (YouTube, Instagram, TikTok, etc.)
- Virality Score (select): "High", "Medium", or "Low"
- Council - External Intelligence (relation): Link to the Council entry of the source speaker
- Related Entities (relation): Link to any Master Framework entries created
- Status (status): "Raw Idea"
- Change Log (rich_text): "[DATE]: Generated from YouTube Pipeline. Source: [Video Title] by [Speaker]."

**Content Idea Generation Rules:**
1. Each idea must be SPECIFIC to the person (Ryan = real estate, authority, family brand; Drey = content strategy, AI, editing, personal brand)
2. Reference the specific framework or quote that inspired the idea
3. Include a hook that could open the video
4. Tag the Virality Score honestly - only "High" if it's genuinely controversial, emotional, or novel
5. Link back to Council member and frameworks via relations
6. Ideas should be filmable - not abstract concepts but specific videos with clear hooks

**Example for Ryan from Lulu Cheng video:**
- Title: "People only remember 3 things about you. Here's how to choose them."
- Hook: "Everyone thinks they control their reputation. They don't. People remember exactly 2-3 things about you - and if you didn't choose those things, someone else did."
- Creator: Ryan
- Bucket: Self-Improvement
- Virality Score: High

**Example for Drey from same video:**
- Title: "The PR expert who saved Activision taught me how cults actually work"
- Hook: "Cult leaders don't have better ideas. They just communicate differently. Here's what the #1 PR expert in America told me about how to make people believe anything."
- Creator: Drey
- Bucket: Controversial Take
- Virality Score: High


### Route 6: AI Prompts Library — "[Speaker] Asks YOU" Action Prompt

**Database ID:** `49007403-0190-4713-9fbc-773733fccb4f`
**Data Source ID:** `848083f6-51a1-44bd-9765-18f32b6359e7`

Every YouTube extraction MUST generate an actionable system prompt that roleplays as the speaker and walks the user through the video's methodology. This is the "Prompt Everything" pattern.

**The "[Speaker] Asks YOU" Bot Pattern:**
1. Roleplay as the speaker using their actual language, quotes, and frameworks
2. Walk the user through 4-6 phases that mirror the video's key teachings
3. Ask probing questions at each phase (the speaker is coaching YOU)
4. Reference actual quotes from the video as the AI responds
5. End with a personalized action plan

**Properties:**
- Prompt Name (title): "[Speaker] Asks YOU — [Topic] Bot" (e.g., "Lulu Asks YOU — Communication Audit Bot")
- Category (select): Appropriate category
- Status (select): "Approved"
- Performance (select): "Not Tested"
- Use Case (multi_select): ["Content Ideas", "Strategy", "Pattern Recognition"]
- Source Video URL (url): YouTube link
- Change Log (rich_text): "[DATE]: Created via YouTube Pipeline. Source: [Video Title]."

**Page Content:**
- The full system prompt as a code block (copy-paste ready for Claude/ChatGPT)
- Quick reference of what the bot does
- Link to source video

**ALSO create a Content & Ideas entry** for Drey called "[Speaker] Asks YOU Bot — [Topic]" following the same 5-Format Content Package pattern used for Tony Asks YOU Bot:
- This IS the "Prompt Everything" content series
- Every video Drey watches generates a shareable prompt
- Content hook: "I watched X → turned it into a prompt → you can use it"
- Include hooks that reference the specific video and speaker
- Position Drey as "The Operationalizer" who turns consumption into tools

**Content Idea Rules for Video-Sourced Ideas:**
1. DIRECTLY reference the specific video ("Lulu said X at timestamp Y")
2. QUOTE the speaker ("As Lulu put it: 'Reality bends to whoever tells the better story'")
3. Reference the "[Speaker] Asks YOU" prompt as the CTA
4. The content idea should make the viewer want to use the prompt

### Route 7: Client Intelligence Routing (Auto-Compound)

**Database ID:** `2b617674-eab4-80c4-8f76-e0c5b5d570e3` (Client Profiles)

Every extraction MUST check: **does this video mention any Viral Editz client?** If yes, route intelligence to their Client Profile. This builds a living, data-backed content formula per client.

**What gets routed to each client mentioned:**

1. **Proven Hooks** (APPEND, never overwrite): What hooks/formats performed + view counts. Data-backed only — no opinions.
2. **Psychology**: What emotional triggers work for this client's audience (disgust, curiosity, fear, hope, anger). Derived from what ACTUALLY went viral, not theory.
3. **Speech Patterns**: How the client talks, filler words to remove, uncertainty phrases, pacing notes, aggressive vs. light editing needed.
4. **Products**: Current products mentioned, packaging changes, spelling warnings for AI transcription errors.
5. **Content Themes**: Topics/angles that have proven performance for this niche.

**The Client Content Formula (build on every extraction):**
```
PROVEN TRIGGERS (Underground) × BRAND VOICE (Machine) × INDUSTRY PATTERNS (Surface)
= Data-Backed Filming Topics
```

**Cross-Reference Rule:** When extracting a video about a niche/industry, check if other clients share the same "message feeling" — not same niche, but same emotional trigger pattern. If Epic Dental works with disgust + curiosity, and another health client could use the same triggers, note the cross-reference.

**Filming Session Generator:** After 3+ extractions for a client, the clone should be able to generate 20 data-backed filming topics for their next quarterly session by combining: proven hooks + proven triggers + untapped angles from the same "message feeling" cluster.

**Luis Clone DB:** `2b617674-eab4-8042-83cb-f370e0c53eb4`
**Ryan Magin** — routes to People DB or dedicated clone profile (same intelligence, Ryan's personality)

Every extraction that contains viral content expertise, editing methodology, client management intelligence, or content creation patterns MUST be routed to BOTH clones:

**Luis Clone gets:**
- Raw decision-making patterns in Luis's voice/language
- His exact verbal patterns, delegation style, quality standards
- Business principles as he states them

**Ryan Clone gets the SAME intelligence but reframed:**
- Same psychology, same frameworks, same "what works and why"
- But described in Ryan's context (viral content strategy, street interviews, personal brand authority)
- Ryan's use case: daily authority content, street interview format, content framework teaching, client results content
- NOTE: Ryan is NOT a real estate brand. He works WITH real estate clients but his expertise is viral content creation and strategy.
- Ryan doesn't need editing SOPs — he needs the STRATEGY layer (what to say, why it works, when to post)

**The overlap:** Both share expertise in viral psychology, storytelling, content strategy, audience building. The Underground layer (WHY things work) is identical. The Surface layer (WHAT gets produced) differs by brand.

**What to route to both:**
- Emotional trigger patterns (timeless, applies to any niche)
- Hook formulas (structure is universal, topic is brand-specific)
- Audience psychology (retention patterns, CTA placement, one-topic rule)
- Content creation frameworks (NRVES principles apply to any creator)

**What's Luis-only:**
- Editing SOPs, VA management, Viral Editz operations
- Client-specific editing instructions
- Agency business model details

**What's Ryan-only:**
- Real estate content applications
- Personal brand authority positioning
- Ryan's specific content topics and audience

## API ACCESS

**Notion API Token:** `ntn_X62823410449D6uWLOLVFYAyo0pQPnxzGGFt59gWUPk8AI`

**When MCP tools fail** (known bug: `mcp__notion__API-post-page` serializes parent parameter to string instead of object, causing `body.parent should be an object` error), use direct API calls:

```python
import json, urllib.request, ssl

TOKEN = "ntn_X62823410449D6uWLOLVFYAyo0pQPnxzGGFt59gWUPk8AI"
HEADERS = {
    'Authorization': f'Bearer {TOKEN}',
    'Content-Type': 'application/json',
    'Notion-Version': '2022-06-28'
}

def notion_create_page(database_id, properties, children=None):
    data = {"parent": {"database_id": database_id}, "properties": properties}
    if children:
        data["children"] = children
    body = json.dumps(data).encode()
    req = urllib.request.Request('https://api.notion.com/v1/pages', data=body, headers=HEADERS, method='POST')
    ctx = ssl.create_default_context()
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=ctx) as resp:
        return json.loads(resp.read())

def notion_append_blocks(page_id, children):
    data = {"children": children}
    body = json.dumps(data).encode()
    req = urllib.request.Request(f'https://api.notion.com/v1/blocks/{page_id}/children', data=body, headers=HEADERS, method='PATCH')
    ctx = ssl.create_default_context()
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=ctx) as resp:
        return json.loads(resp.read())

def notion_update_page(page_id, properties):
    body = json.dumps({"properties": properties}).encode()
    req = urllib.request.Request(f'https://api.notion.com/v1/pages/{page_id}', data=body, headers=HEADERS, method='PATCH')
    ctx = ssl.create_default_context()
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=ctx) as resp:
        return json.loads(resp.read())
```

**Use MCP tools for:** reads, queries, searches, block retrieval.
**Use direct API (curl/Python) for:** page creation, property updates, block appends.

| Database | Title Property |

| Master Frameworks | `Entity Name` |
| Content & Ideas | `Content Title` |

## PROCESSING ORDER

1. Extract metadata (title, channel, duration, description)
2. Attempt transcript extraction (subs → auto-subs → description fallback)
3. Fetch additional context (episode pages, guest profiles)
4. Extract ALL frameworks
5. Pull quotable lines
6. Write applications (Ryan Magin, Drey, Viral Editz)
7. Generate content ideas (3-5 per person: Ryan + Drey)
8. DEDUP CHECK on Council for speaker
9. Create YouTube Library entry (with full page content)
10. Create/update Council entry
11. Create Master Framework entries (one per framework)
12. Create Quotes Library entries (best 3-5)
13. Create Content & Ideas entries (Ryan ideas + Drey ideas)
14. Link Content Ideas → Council member + Framework relations
15. Update Change Log on all entries

- [ ] Content ideas generated for Drey (3-5 ideas)

- [ ] Virality scores are honest (not everything is "High")

**Route 4 (Quotes):**
- [ ] 3-5 quotes minimum
- [ ] Each has: Quote text, Source Person, Source Type, Source Title, Source URL, Vibe tags, Change Log
- [ ] Quotes are actually quotable (stand alone as tweets/hooks)

**Route 5 (Content Ideas):**
- [ ] Minimum 3 for Ryan, minimum 3 for Drey
- [ ] Each has: Hook (specific, not generic), Big Idea, Core Message, Script outline
- [ ] Each references the specific video/speaker (not generic advice)
- [ ] Council relation linked to speaker
- [ ] Virality scores are honest (not all "High")
- [ ] Platform tags make sense for the content type

**Route 6 (Asks YOU Bot):**
- [ ] System prompt is complete (4-6 phases, uses speaker's actual language)
- [ ] Prompt references actual quotes from the video
- [ ] Content idea for Drey exists (the "Prompt Everything" series entry)
- [ ] Source Video URL linked

If ANY checkbox fails → STOP. Fix it before proceeding.


### Pass 3: Cross-Reference Integrity Check
Verify all the pieces link together:

- [ ] YouTube Library entry's Council Link → points to correct Council page
- [ ] Council entry's Linked Frameworks → contains ALL framework page IDs from Route 3
- [ ] Content Ideas entries' "Council - External Intelligence" relation → points to correct Council page
- [ ] Content Ideas entries' "Related Entities" relation → points to relevant Framework entries
- [ ] Extraction Status multi_select on YouTube Library → all 6 routes tagged
- [ ] Change Log on EVERY entry touched includes date + action + source reference
- [ ] Source URL is identical on all entries (no typos, no variations)

If ANY cross-reference is broken → STOP. Fix the link.


### QA Output Format
After all 3 passes, output this table:

```
EXTRACTION QA REPORT: [Video Title]
====================================
PASS 1 - EXISTENCE          PASS 2 - COMPLETENESS       PASS 3 - CROSS-REFS
Route 1: [OK/FAIL]          Route 1: [OK/FAIL]          Links: [OK/FAIL]
Route 2: [OK/FAIL]          Route 2: [OK/FAIL]          Relations: [OK/FAIL]
Route 3: [OK/FAIL] (N/N)    Route 3: [OK/FAIL]          Status Tags: [OK/FAIL]
Route 4: [OK/FAIL] (N/5)    Route 4: [OK/FAIL]          Change Logs: [OK/FAIL]
Route 5: [OK/FAIL] (N/6+)   Route 5: [OK/FAIL]          Source URLs: [OK/FAIL]
Route 6: [OK/FAIL]          Route 6: [OK/FAIL]

VERDICT: [ALL CLEAR / X ISSUES FOUND]
```

Only report "DONE - Fully Routed" to Drey when ALL three passes show OK across all routes.

---


## SAFETY PROTOCOL

- ADDITIVE ONLY. Create pages, add content, append blocks. Never delete or modify existing entries.
- Always include Change Log entry on every database entry touched.
- Always preserve source links.
- Read `luis-brain-hub/references/notion-safety.md` before any write operation.
